result.stdout:
zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll [options]
  dotnet zeroql.dll [command] [...]

OPTIONS
  -h|--help         Shows help text. 
  --version         Shows version information. 

COMMANDS
  config init       Initialize a new configuration file 
  config output     Reads output from config file. Command as workaround for MSBuild inability to read json. 
  generate          Generates C# classes from graphql file. 
  queries extract   Extracts graphql queries from assembly. 
  schema pull       Pulls the schema from a remote server 

You can run `dotnet zeroql.dll [command] --help` to show help on a specific command.

zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll config init [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Initialize a new configuration file

OPTIONS
  -o|--output       Output file path, ./zeroql.json by default Default: "./config.zeroql.json".
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll config output [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Reads output from config file. Command as workaround for MSBuild inability to read json.

OPTIONS
  -c|--config       The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap. 
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

result.stdout:
zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll generate [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Generates C# classes from graphql file.

OPTIONS
  -c|--config       The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap. 
  -s|--schema       The path to get the schema file. For example, './schema.graphql' 
  -n|--namespace    The namespace for generated client 
  -q|--client-name  The client name for the generated client 
  -o|--output       The output file. For example, './Generated/GraphQL.g.cs' 
  -v|--visibility   The visibility within the assembly for the generated client Choices: "Public", "Internal". 
  --warnings-to-ignore  The warnings to ignore when generating the client. Example: --warnings-to-ignore=CS0168,CS0219 
  --scalars         Custom scalars to use when generating the client. Example: --scalars Point=Geometry.Point --scalars Rect=Geometry.Rect 
  --netstandard-compatibility  Enables netstandard compatibility during generation. 
  -f|--force        Ignore checksum check and generate source code Default: "False".
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

  -q|--client-name  The client name for the generated client

  --netstandard-compatibility  Enables netstandard compatibility during generation. 
  -f|--force        Ignore checksum check and generate source code Default: "False".
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

result.stdout:
zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll queries extract --assembly <value> --class <value> --output <value> [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Extracts graphql queries from assembly.

OPTIONS
* -a|--assembly     The assembly that contains the graphql client. For example, './MyApp.dll' 
* -c|--class        The graphql client class name. For example, 'TestGraphQLClient 
* -o|--output       The output folder. For example, './Queries' 
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

result.stdout:
zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll schema pull [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Pulls the schema from a remote server

OPTIONS
  -c|--config       The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap. 
  -u|--url          The url to pull the schema from. For example, https://server.com/graphql 
  -o|--output       The output file. For example, './schema.graphql' Default: "schema.graphql".
  -t|--token        Access Token to use when downloading the schema 
  -a|--auth         Auth scheme to use when downloading the schema 
  -x|--headers      Custom headers to use when downloading the schema. Example: --headers key1=value1 --headers key2=value2 
  --timeout         Timeout in seconds for downloading the schema. Default is 30 seconds 
  -h|--help         Shows help text.

commands.payload:
zeroql v8.1.0

USAGE
  dotnet zeroql.dll schema pull [options]

DESCRIPTION
  Pulls the schema from a remote server

OPTIONS
  -c|--config       The generation config file. For example, './zeroql.json'. Use `zeroql config init` to bootstrap. 
  -u|--url          The url to pull the schema from. For example, https://server.com/graphql 
  -o|--output       The output file. For example, './schema.graphql' Default: "schema.graphql".
  -t|--token        Access Token to use when downloading the schema 
  -a|--auth         Auth scheme to use when downloading the schema 
  -x|--headers      Custom headers to use when downloading the schema. Example: --headers key1=value1 --headers key2=value2 
  --timeout         Timeout in seconds for downloading the schema. Default is 30 seconds 
  -h|--help         Shows help text.
